supply_chain.dependencies: Many components used in manufacturing are readily fabricated from commonly available raw materials or off-the-shelf items from multiple supply sources; however, certain items are custom made to meet specifications. Certain materials and components are purchased from single sources due to quality considerations, expertise, costs or constraints from regulatory requirements.
supply_chain.risks: Disruptions in supply of materials and components used in manufacturing products or sterilization of products could adversely affect results. Uncertain or negative economic conditions could negatively affect third-party vendors, leading to reduction or interruption in supply or increased prices. Predictability in supply of certain raw materials and components continues to be a risk. Supply chain dynamics could result in increased costs or inability to fully meet customer demand for certain products. Labor shortages and inflation impact on cost of raw materials and wage costs. Natural disasters, climate change, public health crises, or geopolitical developments may cause disruptions to manufacturing, distribution, and supply chains.

regulatory_risks: Extensive and dynamic medical device regulation by FDA and comparable international agencies may impede or hinder approval or sale of products
EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR) implementation costs and compliance requirements
Increasingly stringent and unpredictable global regulatory environment increasing time, cost and complexity of obtaining regulatory approvals
Several countries establishing new regulatory requirements for medical devices or expanding existing regulations
Certain regulators requiring local preclinical and clinical data in addition to global data
Risk of FDA warning letters, field actions, or similar actions by regulatory agencies relating to products and processes

Increased scrutiny and heightened global regulatory enforcement facing medical device industry under U.S. Anti-Kickback Statute, U.S. False Claims Act, U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and similar laws in other jurisdictions
U.S. and foreign export control, trade embargo and customs laws compliance
Health care policy changes and legislative or regulatory efforts in U.S., EU and worldwide to modify product approval or reimbursement processes
Trend toward demonstrating clinical outcomes, comparative effectiveness and cost efficiency
Changes in tax laws, unfavorable resolution of tax contingencies, or exposure to additional income tax liabilities
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax framework adoption by many countries
Potential U.S. tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, China or other countries
Legal and regulatory requirements for data privacy including GDPR in Europe
Environmental laws and regulations including regulations on use of ethylene oxide and PFAS materials
Climate change related regulations and disclosure requirements

sanction_risks: Sanctions and export restrictions may proliferate leading to greater uncertainty in emerging and growth markets
Russia/Ukraine war has created barriers to doing business in Russia and parts of Eastern Europe
Tension between China/Taiwan has created geopolitical shifts in Asia
Conflicts in Middle East have disrupted operations of companies doing business in region including Israel
Increased cybersecurity risks as indirect result of ongoing Russia/Ukraine war and conflicts in Middle East
Potential impact of sanctions on U.S. manufacturers doing business in certain regions
FCPA investigations related to operations in Vietnam and potential concerns in other countries
